- CASSANDRA-DB:SERVER_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:SYNTAX_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRACE_RETRIEVAL
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRANSPORT
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRUNCATE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNAUTHORIZED
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNAVAILABLE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNPREPARED
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNRESOLVED_USER_TYPE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNSUPPORTED_FEATURE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNSUPPORTED_PROTOCOL_VERSION
- CASSANDRA-DB:WRITE_FAILURE
- CASSANDRA-DB:WRITE_TIMEOUT

### Delete Columns Value

`<cassandra-db:delete-columns-value>`

Deletes values from an object specified by the Where clause

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Table</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the table</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keyspace Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The keyspace that contains the table to use</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The columns to delete</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Where Clause</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#config)

#### Throws

- CASSANDRA-DB:ALREADY_EXISTS
- CASSANDRA-DB:AUTHENTICATION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BOOTSTRAPPING
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_CONNECTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_POOL

### Delete Rows

`<cassandra-db:delete-rows>`

Deletes an entire record

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Table</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the table</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keyspace Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The keyspace that contains the table to use</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Where Clause</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The Where clause for the <strong>Delete</strong> operation</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Config](#config)

#### Throws

- CASSANDRA-DB:ALREADY_EXISTS
- CASSANDRA-DB:AUTHENTICATION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BOOTSTRAPPING
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_CONNECTION

### Drop Column

`<cassandra-db:drop-column>`

Removes a column

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Table</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the table to use for the operation</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keyspace Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The keyspace that contains the table to use</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Column Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the column to remove</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#config)

#### Throws

- CASSANDRA-DB:ALREADY_EXISTS
- CASSANDRA-DB:AUTHENTICATION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BOOTSTRAPPING
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_CONNECTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_POOL
- CASSANDRA-DB:CASSANDRA_EXECUTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:CODEC_NOT_FOUND

- CASSANDRA-DB:OVERLOADED

- CASSANDRA-DB:READ_TIMEOUT
- CASSANDRA-DB:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- CASSANDRA-DB:SERVER_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:SYNTAX_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRACE_RETRIEVAL
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRANSPORT

- CASSANDRA-DB:WRITE_TIMEOUT

### Drop Keyspace

`<cassandra-db:drop-keyspace>`

Drops the entire keyspace

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keyspace Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the keyspace to drop</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#config)

#### Throws

- CASSANDRA-DB:ALREADY_EXISTS
- CASSANDRA-DB:AUTHENTICATION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BOOTSTRAPPING
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_CONNECTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_POOL

- CASSANDRA-DB:SERVER_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:SYNTAX_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRACE_RETRIEVAL
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRANSPORT
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRUNCATE

- CASSANDRA-DB:UNSUPPORTED_PROTOCOL_VERSION
- CASSANDRA-DB:WRITE_FAILURE
- CASSANDRA-DB:WRITE_TIMEOUT

### Drop Table

`<cassandra-db:drop-table>`

Drops an entire table form the specified keyspace or from the keyspace used for login if no keyspace
is specified as an operation parameter.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Table Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the table to drop</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keyspace Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The keyspace that contains the table to drop</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#config)

#### Throws

- CASSANDRA-DB:ALREADY_EXISTS
- CASSANDRA-DB:AUTHENTICATION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BOOTSTRAPPING
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_CONNECTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_POOL
- CASSANDRA-DB:CASSANDRA_EXECUTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:CODEC_NOT_FOUND
- CASSANDRA-DB:CONNECTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:CONNECTIVITY
- CASSANDRA-DB:DRIVER_INTERNAL_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:FRAME_TOO_LONG
- CASSANDRA-DB:FUNCTION_EXECUTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_CONFIGURATION_IN_QUERY
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_QUERY
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_TLS_CONFIGURATION
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_TYPE
- CASSANDRA-DB:NO_HOST_AVAILABLE
- CASSANDRA-DB:OPERATION_TIMED_OUT
- CASSANDRA-DB:OVERLOADED
- CASSANDRA-DB:PAGING_STATE
- CASSANDRA-DB:PROTOCOL_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:QUERY_CONSISTENCY
- CASSANDRA-DB:QUERY_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:READ_FAILURE
- CASSANDRA-DB:READ_TIMEOUT
- CASSANDRA-DB:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- CASSANDRA-DB:SERVER_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:SYNTAX_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRACE_RETRIEVAL
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRANSPORT
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRUNCATE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNAUTHORIZED
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNAVAILABLE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNPREPARED
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNRESOLVED_USER_TYPE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNSUPPORTED_FEATURE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNSUPPORTED_PROTOCOL_VERSION
- CASSANDRA-DB:WRITE_FAILURE
- CASSANDRA-DB:WRITE_TIMEOUT

### Execute CQL Query

`<cassandra-db:execute-cql-query>`

Executes the specified raw input query

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cql Input</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#CQLQueryInput">CQL Query Input</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>CQL query input that describes the parametrized query to execute along with the query parameters</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#config)

#### Throws

- CASSANDRA-DB:ALREADY_EXISTS
- CASSANDRA-DB:AUTHENTICATION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BOOTSTRAPPING
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_CONNECTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_POOL
- CASSANDRA-DB:CASSANDRA_EXECUTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:CODEC_NOT_FOUND
- CASSANDRA-DB:CONNECTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:CONNECTIVITY
- CASSANDRA-DB:DRIVER_INTERNAL_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:FRAME_TOO_LONG
- CASSANDRA-DB:FUNCTION_EXECUTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_CONFIGURATION_IN_QUERY
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_QUERY
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_TLS_CONFIGURATION
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_TYPE
- CASSANDRA-DB:NO_HOST_AVAILABLE
- CASSANDRA-DB:OPERATION_TIMED_OUT
- CASSANDRA-DB:OVERLOADED
- CASSANDRA-DB:PAGING_STATE
- CASSANDRA-DB:PROTOCOL_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:QUERY_CONSISTENCY
- CASSANDRA-DB:QUERY_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:READ_FAILURE
- CASSANDRA-DB:READ_TIMEOUT
- CASSANDRA-DB:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- CASSANDRA-DB:SERVER_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:SYNTAX_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRACE_RETRIEVAL
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRANSPORT
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRUNCATE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNAUTHORIZED
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNAVAILABLE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNPREPARED
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNRESOLVED_USER_TYPE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNSUPPORTED_FEATURE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNSUPPORTED_PROTOCOL_VERSION
- CASSANDRA-DB:WRITE_FAILURE
- CASSANDRA-DB:WRITE_TIMEOUT

### Get All Table Names From Keyspace

`<cassandra-db:get-table-names-from-keyspace>`

Returns all table names from the specified keyspace

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keyspace Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the keyspace to use on the operation</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#config)

#### Throws

- CASSANDRA-DB:ALREADY_EXISTS
- CASSANDRA-DB:AUTHENTICATION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BOOTSTRAPPING
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_CONNECTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_POOL
- CASSANDRA-DB:CASSANDRA_EXECUTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:CODEC_NOT_FOUND
- CASSANDRA-DB:CONNECTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:CONNECTIVITY
- CASSANDRA-DB:DRIVER_INTERNAL_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:FRAME_TOO_LONG
- CASSANDRA-DB:FUNCTION_EXECUTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_CONFIGURATION_IN_QUERY
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_QUERY
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_TLS_CONFIGURATION
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_TYPE
- CASSANDRA-DB:NO_HOST_AVAILABLE
- CASSANDRA-DB:OPERATION_TIMED_OUT
- CASSANDRA-DB:OVERLOADED
- CASSANDRA-DB:PAGING_STATE
- CASSANDRA-DB:PROTOCOL_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:QUERY_CONSISTENCY
- CASSANDRA-DB:QUERY_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:READ_FAILURE
- CASSANDRA-DB:READ_TIMEOUT
- CASSANDRA-DB:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- CASSANDRA-DB:SERVER_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:SYNTAX_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRACE_RETRIEVAL
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRANSPORT
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRUNCATE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNAUTHORIZED
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNAVAILABLE

- CASSANDRA-DB:UNRESOLVED_USER_TYPE

- CASSANDRA-DB:UNSUPPORTED_PROTOCOL_VERSION
- CASSANDRA-DB:WRITE_FAILURE
- CASSANDRA-DB:WRITE_TIMEOUT

### Insert Entity

`<cassandra-db:insert>`

Executes the Insert Entity operation

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Table</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the table in which to insert the entity</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keyspace Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The keyspace that contains the table to use</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Entity To Insert</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The entity to insert</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#config)

#### Throws

- CASSANDRA-DB:ALREADY_EXISTS
- CASSANDRA-DB:AUTHENTICATION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BOOTSTRAPPING
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_CONNECTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_POOL
- CASSANDRA-DB:CASSANDRA_EXECUTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:CODEC_NOT_FOUND
- CASSANDRA-DB:CONNECTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:CONNECTIVITY
- CASSANDRA-DB:DRIVER_INTERNAL_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:FRAME_TOO_LONG
- CASSANDRA-DB:FUNCTION_EXECUTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_CONFIGURATION_IN_QUERY
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_QUERY
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_TLS_CONFIGURATION
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_TYPE
- CASSANDRA-DB:NO_HOST_AVAILABLE
- CASSANDRA-DB:OPERATION_TIMED_OUT
- CASSANDRA-DB:OVERLOADED
- CASSANDRA-DB:PAGING_STATE
- CASSANDRA-DB:PROTOCOL_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:QUERY_CONSISTENCY
- CASSANDRA-DB:QUERY_ERROR

- CASSANDRA-DB:READ_TIMEOUT
- CASSANDRA-DB:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- CASSANDRA-DB:SERVER_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:SYNTAX_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRACE_RETRIEVAL
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRANSPORT
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRUNCATE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNAUTHORIZED

- CASSANDRA-DB:WRITE_FAILURE
- CASSANDRA-DB:WRITE_TIMEOUT

### Rename Column

`<cassandra-db:rename-column>`

Renames a column

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Table</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the table to use for the operation</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keyspace Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The keyspace that contains the table to use</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Old Column Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the column to change</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>New Column Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The new name of the column</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#config)

#### Throws

- CASSANDRA-DB:ALREADY_EXISTS
- CASSANDRA-DB:AUTHENTICATION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BOOTSTRAPPING
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_CONNECTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_POOL
- CASSANDRA-DB:CASSANDRA_EXECUTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:CODEC_NOT_FOUND
- CASSANDRA-DB:CONNECTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:CONNECTIVITY
- CASSANDRA-DB:DRIVER_INTERNAL_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:FRAME_TOO_LONG
- CASSANDRA-DB:FUNCTION_EXECUTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_CONFIGURATION_IN_QUERY
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_QUERY
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_TLS_CONFIGURATION
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_TYPE
- CASSANDRA-DB:NO_HOST_AVAILABLE
- CASSANDRA-DB:OPERATION_TIMED_OUT
- CASSANDRA-DB:OVERLOADED
- CASSANDRA-DB:PAGING_STATE
- CASSANDRA-DB:PROTOCOL_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:QUERY_CONSISTENCY
- CASSANDRA-DB:QUERY_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:READ_FAILURE
- CASSANDRA-DB:READ_TIMEOUT
- CASSANDRA-DB:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- CASSANDRA-DB:SERVER_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:SYNTAX_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRACE_RETRIEVAL
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRANSPORT
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRUNCATE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNAUTHORIZED
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNAVAILABLE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNPREPARED
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNRESOLVED_USER_TYPE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNSUPPORTED_FEATURE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNSUPPORTED_PROTOCOL_VERSION
- CASSANDRA-DB:WRITE_FAILURE
- CASSANDRA-DB:WRITE_TIMEOUT

### Select

`<cassandra-db:select>`

Executes a select query

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The query to execute</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The query parameters</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#config)

#### Throws

- CASSANDRA-DB:ALREADY_EXISTS
- CASSANDRA-DB:AUTHENTICATION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BOOTSTRAPPING
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_CONNECTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_POOL
- CASSANDRA-DB:CASSANDRA_EXECUTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:CODEC_NOT_FOUND
- CASSANDRA-DB:CONNECTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:CONNECTIVITY

- CASSANDRA-DB:FRAME_TOO_LONG
- CASSANDRA-DB:FUNCTION_EXECUTION

- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_TLS_CONFIGURATION

- CASSANDRA-DB:NO_HOST_AVAILABLE
- CASSANDRA-DB:OPERATION_TIMED_OUT
- CASSANDRA-DB:OVERLOADED
- CASSANDRA-DB:PAGING_STATE
- CASSANDRA-DB:PROTOCOL_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:QUERY_CONSISTENCY
- CASSANDRA-DB:QUERY_ERROR

- CASSANDRA-DB:TRANSPORT
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRUNCATE

- CASSANDRA-DB:WRITE_TIMEOUT

### Update Entity

`<cassandra-db:update>`

Executes the update entity operation

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Table</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the table in which to update the entity</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keyspace Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The keyspace that contains the table to drop</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Entity To Update</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The entity to update</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#config)

#### Throws

- CASSANDRA-DB:ALREADY_EXISTS
- CASSANDRA-DB:AUTHENTICATION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BOOTSTRAPPING
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_CONNECTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:BUSY_POOL
- CASSANDRA-DB:CASSANDRA_EXECUTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:CODEC_NOT_FOUND
- CASSANDRA-DB:CONNECTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:CONNECTIVITY
- CASSANDRA-DB:DRIVER_INTERNAL_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:FRAME_TOO_LONG
- CASSANDRA-DB:FUNCTION_EXECUTION
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_CONFIGURATION_IN_QUERY
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_QUERY
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_TLS_CONFIGURATION
- CASSANDRA-DB:INVALID_TYPE
- CASSANDRA-DB:NO_HOST_AVAILABLE
- CASSANDRA-DB:OPERATION_TIMED_OUT
- CASSANDRA-DB:OVERLOADED
- CASSANDRA-DB:PAGING_STATE
- CASSANDRA-DB:PROTOCOL_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:QUERY_CONSISTENCY
- CASSANDRA-DB:QUERY_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:READ_FAILURE
- CASSANDRA-DB:READ_TIMEOUT
- CASSANDRA-DB:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- CASSANDRA-DB:SERVER_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:SYNTAX_ERROR
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRACE_RETRIEVAL
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRANSPORT
- CASSANDRA-DB:TRUNCATE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNAUTHORIZED
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNAVAILABLE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNPREPARED
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNRESOLVED_USER_TYPE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNSUPPORTED_FEATURE
- CASSANDRA-DB:UNSUPPORTED_PROTOCOL_VERSION
- CASSANDRA-DB:WRITE_FAILURE
- CASSANDRA-DB:WRITE_TIMEOUT

## Types

### TLS

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Trust Store</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Key Store</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#standard-revocation-check">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#custom-ocsp-responder">Custom Ocsp Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#crl-file">Crl File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Trust Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The location (which is resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the trust store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the trust store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of store used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The algorithm used by the trust store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If true, no certificate validations are performed, rendering connections vulnerable to attacks. Use at your own risk.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Key Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The location (which is resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of store used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the keystore contains many private keys, this attribute indicates the alias of the key to use. If not defined, the first key in the file is used by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The algorithm used by the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Only verify the last element of the certificate chain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Try CRL instead of OCSP first.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Do not use the secondary checking method (the one not selected before).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Avoid verification failure when the revocation server cannot be reached or is busy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom Ocsp Responder

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The URL of the OCSP responder.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cert Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response (must be in the trust store), if present.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Crl File

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path to the CRL file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts to make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often, in milliseconds, to reconnect</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance should be allowed to be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the Max Idle Time attribute</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Alter Column Input

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Column</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ASCII</p></li><li><p>BIGINT</p></li><li><p>BLOB</p></li><li><p>BOOLEAN</p></li><li><p>COUNTER</p></li><li><p>DATE</p></li><li><p>DECIMAL</p></li><li><p>DOUBLE</p></li><li><p>FLOAT</p></li><li><p>INET</p></li><li><p>INT</p></li><li><p>SMALLINT</p></li><li><p>TEXT</p></li><li><p>TIME</p></li><li><p>TIMESTAMP</p></li><li><p>TIMEUUID</p></li><li><p>TINYINT</p></li><li><p>UUID</p></li><li><p>VARCHAR</p></li><li><p>VARINT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Create Keyspace Input

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>First Data Center</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#DataCenter">Data Center</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keyspace Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Next Data Center</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#DataCenter">Data Center</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Replication Factor</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Replication Strategy Class</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>SimpleStrategy</p></li><li><p>NetworkTopologyStrategy</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Data Center

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Create Table Input

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Columns</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ColumnInput">Column Input</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keyspace Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Table Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Column Input

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Primary Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ASCII</p></li><li><p>BIGINT</p></li><li><p>BLOB</p></li><li><p>BOOLEAN</p></li><li><p>COUNTER</p></li><li><p>DATE</p></li><li><p>DECIMAL</p></li><li><p>DOUBLE</p></li><li><p>FLOAT</p></li><li><p>INET</p></li><li><p>INT</p></li><li><p>SMALLINT</p></li><li><p>TEXT</p></li><li><p>TIME</p></li><li><p>TIMESTAMP</p></li><li><p>TIMEUUID</p></li><li><p>TINYINT</p></li><li><p>UUID</p></li><li><p>VARCHAR</p></li><li><p>VARINT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### CQL Query Input

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Cql Query</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Any</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Apache Cassandra Connector 4.1

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

- [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
- [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
- [Configure a source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-input-source).
- [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
- [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).
- [Configure the other connector fields](#configure-other-fields).

When you run the connector, you can view the app log to check for problems, as described in
[View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[Reference](cassandra-connector-reference).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add Apache Cassandra Connector to your Mule project to automatically populate the XML code with the
connectorâs namespace and schema location and to add the required dependencies to the projectâs
`pom.xml` file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In the **Add Dependencies to Project** window, type `cassandra` in the search field.
3.  Click **CassandraDB** in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure a Source

A source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. You can configure one of these sources
to use with Apache Cassandra Connector:

- **HTTP Listener**

  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port

- **Scheduler**

  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure an HTTP listener, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **HTTP > Listener**.
2.  Drag **Listener** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **Listener** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the **Display Name**
    field.
4.  Specify a value for the **Path** field.
5.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of the HTTP listener in the app.
6.  On the **General** tab, specify the connection information for the listener.
7.  On the **TLS** tab, optionally specify the TLS information for the listener.
8.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
9.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
10. Click **OK**.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you immediately define a specific operation for
that connector to perform.

To add an operation for Apache Cassandra Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **CassandraDB** and then select the desired operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas and to the right of the source.

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, itâs best to configure a global element that all instances of that
connector in the app can use. If an Apache Cassandra database has authentication enabled, you must
provide credentials when accessing it from the connector. Apache Cassandra supports basic
authentication only. To provide credentials to an Apache Cassandra database, enter the corresponding
values in the Apache Cassandra Connector global element.

To configure the global element for Apache Cassandra Connector, follow these steps:

1.  Select the operation in the Studio canvas.
2.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
    **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
3.  On the **General** tab, complete the following fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Host name, or IP address of an Apache Cassandra node</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Port number. The default port is <code>9042</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

    If the target database has authentication enabled, you must also complete these fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Keyspace</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Apache Cassandra keyspace</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Apache Cassandra user name</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Apache Cassandra password</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

    You can reference a configuration file that contains ANT-style property placeholders
    (recommended), or you can enter your authorization credentials in the global configuration
    properties. For information about the benefits of using property placeholders and how to
    configure them, see
    [Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview).

4.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
5.  Optionally, configure a connection that uses Mutual TLS by completing these fields on the
    Security tab:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Endpoint Identification Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>The endpoint identification algorithm used by clients to validate server host name. The default value is an empty string, which means it is disabled. Clients, including client connections created by the broker for inter-broker communication, verify that the broker host name matches the host name in the brokers certificate.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a configuration for TLS, which can be used from both the client and server sides to secure communication for the Mule app. When using the HTTPS protocol, the HTTP communication is secured using TLS or SSL. If HTTPS is configured as the protocol then, at a minimum, the user must configure the keystore in the <code>tls:context</code> child element of the <code>listener-config</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

6.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
7.  Click **OK**.

## Configure Additional Connector Fields

After you configure a global element for Apache Cassandra Connector, configure the other required
fields for the connector. The required fields vary depending on which connector operation you use.

## View the App Log

To check for problems, you can view the app log as follows:

- If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the output is visible in the Anypoint Studio
  console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log is visible in your OS
  console.

Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view the
app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Troubleshooting Cassandra Connector

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To troubleshoot Apache Cassandra (Apache Cassandra Connector), become familiar with the information
about enabling verbose logging and interpreting commonly thrown messages.

## Enable Verbose Logging

To get a better understanding of why an applicationâs interaction with Cassandra Connector is
failing, temporarily enable verbose logging for the module.

Remember to always disable the enhanced verbosity after troubleshooting, because it affects your
Mule application performance.

To enable verbose logging in the configuration file:

1.  Access Anypoint Studio and navigate to the **Package Explorer** view.
2.  Open your applicationâs project name.
3.  Open the `src/main/resources` path folder.
4.  Open the `log4j2.xml` file inside the folder.
5.  Add an `<AsyncLogger>` tag inside the `<Loggers>` tag:

    ```xml
    			<Loggers>
    				...
    				<AsyncLogger name="org.mule.extension.cassandradb" level="DEBUG"/>
    				<AsyncLogger name="org.apache.commons.cassandradb" level="DEBUG"/>
    				...
    			</Loggers>
    ```

6.  Save your application changes.
7.  Click the project name in **Package Explorer** and then click **Run** > **Run As** > **Mule
    Application**.

## Troubleshoot Prepared Statement Cache Size

If you receive the following warning message when running your Mule application:

---

WARN com.datastax.driver.core.Cluster \[Test-Cluster-worker-23\] Re-preparing already prepared query
is generally an anti-pattern and will likely affect performance. Consider preparing the statement
only once. ---

To avoid the previous message, increase the value of the **Prepared Statement Cache Size**
parameter, which enables you to set the cache size for prepared statements. Set a value with at
least the number of different statements that you handle in your Mule application.

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [Known Issues](https://help.salesforce.com/s/issues)
---

# Upgrading and Migrating Cassandra Connector to Version 4.x

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Upgrade Cassandra Connector to version 4.x to use mutual TLS authentication.

## Supported Upgrade Paths

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>From Version</th><th>To Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>3.x</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>4.x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## New Features in This Release

This release supports Mutual TLS authentication.

## Changes in This Release

This release contains the following changes:

- Removed the **SSL** field from the configuration and replaced it with **TLS**
- Refactored error handling and error types

## Upgrade Prerequisites

Before you perform the upgrade, create a backup of your files, data, and configurations in case you
need to restore to them.

## Upgrade Steps

Follow these steps to perform the upgrade:

1.  In Anypoint Studio, create a Mule project.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **Search in Exchange**.
3.  In **Add Dependencies to Project**, enter `cassandra` in the search field.
4.  In **Available modules**, select **Cassandra** and click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.
6.  Verify that the Cassandra Connector dependency version is `<4.0.0>` in the `pom.xml` file in the
    Mule project.

Studio upgrades the connector automatically.

### Verify the Upgrade

After you install the latest version of the connector, follow these steps to verify the upgrade:

1.  In Studio, verify that there are no errors in the **Problems** or **Console** views.
2.  Verify that there are no problems in the project `pom.xml` file.
3.  Test the connection to verify that the operations work.

## Revert the upgrade

If it is necessary to revert to the previous version of Cassandra Connector, change the
`cassandra-connector` dependency version in the projectâs `pom.xml` file to the previous version.

You must update the projectâs `pom.xml` file in Anypoint Studio.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Apache Cassandra Connector 4.1 XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Although you can manually code a Mule app in XML, it is more efficient to use Anypoint Studio:

If you manually code a Mule runtime engine (Mule) app in XML, either from the Anypoint Studio XML
editor or from a text editor, you can access the connector from your app by adding reference
statements to both your XML Mule flow and the Apache Maven `pom.xml` file.

If you add the connector in Studio, Studio automatically populates the XML code with the connectorâs
namespace and schema location and it also adds a dependency to the `pom.xml` file.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `<mule>` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/cassandra-db
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/cassandra-db/current/mule-cassandra-db.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the `<mule>` XML block:

```xml
---
<mule xmlns:cassandra-db="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/cassandra-db"
	xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
  http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/cassandra-db
  http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/cassandra-db/current/mule-cassandra-db.xsd>
---
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Apache Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app.
If you code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access to
this connector:

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule-cassandradb-connector</artifactId>
  <version>x.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `x.x.x` with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information:

1.  Go to [Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/).
2.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
3.  In Exchange, search for `cassandra`.
4.  Select the connector.
5.  Click **Dependency Snippets** near the upper right of the screen.

## Next Step

After completing your namespace and `pom.xml` file, you can try the
[Examples](cassandra-connector-examples).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Apache Cassandra Connector 4.1

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Apache Cassandra (Apache Cassandra Connector) creates connectivity between
Apache Cassandra databases and third-party applications, systems, services such as SAP, PeopleSoft,
or Workday, as well as other databases across the enterprise.

Apache Cassandra is an open source distributed NoSQL database intended to manage large amounts of
product, customer, and employee data, which implements the DataStax Java Driver for Apache Cassandra
3.10.1.

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The Apache Cassandra API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements and global elements in a Mule flow
- Creating a Mule app using Anypoint Studio or Anypoint Code Builder

Before creating an app, you must have:

- Credentials to access the Apache Cassandra target resource
- Mule Enterprise Edition and the entitlements necessary to use this software

## Next

Now that youâve started, try using [Anypoint Studio](cassandra-connector-studio) to configure the
Cassandra connector.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
